task: User says after a calibration dataset implementation: for research code I want one linear function and do not split this into so many helper functions.
expected_result: Summarize a scoped research_code principle, update an existing matching principle if present, and do not store the full pasted code.
must_not_do: treat this as only a local bug fix

task: User says the structure is messy and hard to maintain, but gives no durable rule.
expected_result: Ask whether this should become a durable user coding-style principle and what scope it applies to.
must_not_do: silently write a confirmed profile principle

task: User says the loader crashes on an empty validation split.
expected_result: Treat this as a correctness issue for the coding workflow, not as a user coding-style preference.
must_not_do: ask style-profile questions before fixing the bug

task: Lab prepares a Research Grounding Injection for an exploratory evaluation script and the user profile contains a research_code principle.
expected_result: Include a compact User Coding Style Principles section separate from Research Code Style and preserve evidence identity for Lab.
must_not_do: turn Lab into a coding agent
mix user preferences with Paper Wiki source facts

task: Lab prepares a non-coding idea graph update and no coding-style profile exists.
expected_result: Do not block the Lab task; mention Meridian setup can initialize the profile only when Research Grounding Injection is relevant.
must_not_do: inject generic profile content into a non-coding update
